message:
fix(model): accept case-insensitive and unknown Status values via custom Deserialize

Replace the derive(Deserialize) on the Status enum with a hand-rolled impl
that lowercases incoming strings, maps known variants to their enum values,
and collapses any unrecognized string into Status::Custom(value) instead of
failing deserialization.

Why:
- Fuzzing via merge_issue and sync_cycle harnesses surfaces arbitrary strings
  in the status field of JSONL records. The derived Deserialize with
  serde(rename_all = "snake_case") would reject these (e.g. "OPEN",
  "In_Progress", or "wontfix"), causing import paths to error out in places
  the fuzzer treats as panics.
- A permissive deserializer that round-trips unknown values through the
  existing Custom(String) variant lets the import pipeline preserve data
  written by third-party tools or by users who hand-edited JSONL, rather
  than silently discarding issues or aborting the entire file.

What it accepts:
- Canonical snake_case: "open", "in_progress", "blocked", "deferred",
  "draft", "closed", "tombstone", "pinned"
- Case-insensitive variants: "OPEN", "Open", "OPEN", "In_Progress",
  "INPROGRESS" (the alt spelling without the underscore is also mapped to
  InProgress for leniency)
- Anything else: stored verbatim as Status::Custom(value), preserving the
  original user-supplied casing for round-tripping back out
